name: Docs
description: A drive for your important documents — leases, IDs, warranties, tax forms — as a projection of the vault. A document is a core.document wrapper around a sha256-deduped canonical content item (issue #352): identity is separate from bytes, so text documents edit in place and any document's content can be replaced, each change a walkable version in its history. Folders are SKOS concepts in the owner's folders scheme, and filing is a single tag per document. Starring is a flags-scheme tag on the same wrapper, so a photo favorited in Photos shows in Starred here. Trash sets a purge date about 30 days out and keeps the folder tag (and the star), so a restored document lands right back where it was. Uploading, renaming, moving, editing, replacing, restoring a version, starring, trashing and folder management all run through typed vault commands — consent-checked and receipted. No data of its own — revoke the grant and the app goes dark.

vault.why: Keeps your important documents filed, editable, downloadable and restorable — with a version history.

actions.name: upload
actions.description: Add a document through core.add_document; the bytes become a deduped canonical content item wrapped in a fresh core.document, filed into a folder (or the drive's top level). Two documents may legitimately share identical bytes — dedup is on the bytes, never on document identity.

actions.name: rename
actions.description: Rename a document through core.rename_document (refuses trashed documents). Renames the wrapper's title only — bytes and version history are untouched.

actions.name: move
actions.description: Refile a document into a folder through core.move_document — omit folder_id to move it to the drive's top level.

actions.name: trash
actions.description: Move a document to the trash through core.trash_document; it keeps its folder and auto-purges about 30 days later. Its current bytes AND every superseded version stay live until the document itself purges.

actions.name: restore
actions.description: Bring a document back from the trash through core.restore_document; it returns to the folder it was filed in.

actions.name: star
actions.description: Star a document through core.star_document — one flags-scheme tag on the document wrapper, the same star every other surface reads. Idempotent; refuses trashed documents.

actions.name: unstar
actions.description: Remove a document's star through core.unstar_document. Idempotent; refuses trashed documents (a trashed document keeps its star through restore).

actions.name: edit
actions.description: Edit a text-editable document's body in place through core.edit_document (media_type text/* only — anything else refuses; use replace instead). Mints a new (or deduped) content item and records the revises link itself; title is an optional partial update.

actions.name: replace
actions.description: Replace a document's bytes (any media type) through core.replace_document_content — the current door for scanned PDFs, images and anything else edit can't touch structurally. Mints a new content item, records the revises link, and repoints the wrapper's current version; title is an optional partial update. Refuses trashed documents.

actions.name: restore-version
actions.description: Restore an older version through core.restore_document_version — history never rewrites (rule R3): this asserts a NEW forward revises link and repoints current_content_id at the chosen past content item, so the old chain stays exactly as it was and a restore-of-a-restore never loops.

actions.name: create-folder
actions.description: Create a folder (a SKOS concept in the folders scheme) through core.create_folder; refuses duplicate sibling names.

actions.name: rename-folder
actions.description: Rename a folder through core.rename_folder (the drive's top level refuses).

actions.name: delete-folder
actions.description: Delete an empty folder through core.delete_folder — refused while it still holds documents (including trashed ones) or subfolders.

queries.name: drive
queries.description: The drive as a bounded recent window: every folder in the owner's folders scheme, plus the most recently filed documents (core.document wrappers carrying a folders-scheme tag) newest first — caller-sized via limit, default 200 — trashed ones included with their purge dates, each row decorated with starred (a flags-scheme tag on the wrapper) and the current content's media_type/byte_size joined by current_content_id. truncated=true means older documents exist beyond the window; grow limit or use the search query to reach them. Version history is a separate read (the history query) — this projection only ever ships the current version.

queries.name: search
queries.description: Full-text document search through the vault's FTS5 index (title + the current version's decoded text body) — returns only the ranked matches in the drive's document row shape plus a hit snippet, never a whole-table pull. Only wrappers carrying a folders-scheme tag come back; trashed documents never match.

queries.name: history
queries.description: A document's version chain, walked from its current content backward through live `revises` core.link edges — never a command, since core.link is already the durable history. Ordered newest-first by ASSERTION time (when a version stopped being current, or its own upload time for the oldest entry), not the content item's own created_at: restoring an old version reads as the newest entry even though its bytes are old.
